flashcards.a: A closed geometric figure bounded exactly by three intersecting straight lines.

flashcards.q: What defines the congruence of two triangles?
flashcards.a: Two triangles are congruent if they are identical in both shape and size, meaning all corresponding sides and angles are equal.

flashcards.q: What does the abbreviation CPCT stand for?
What is the SAS congruence axiom?
flashcards.a: Two triangles are congruent if two sides and the explicitly included angle of one exactly equal the corresponding two sides and included angle of the other.

flashcards.q: Why are the configurations ASS or SSA mathematically invalid for proving congruence?
flashcards.a: Because a non-included angle paired with two sides does not uniquely lock the rigid structural shape of the triangle.

flashcards.q: What is the ASA congruence theorem?
flashcards.a: Two triangles are congruent if two angles and the explicitly included side of one exactly equal the corresponding two angles and included side of the other.
A triangle in which at least two of its bounding sides are completely equal in length.

flashcards.q: State the key property relating to the angles of an isosceles triangle.
flashcards.a: The interior angles traversing directly opposite to the exactly equal side lengths are always equal.

flashcards.q: State the key mathematical property relating to the sides of an isosceles triangle.
flashcards.a: The sides traversing directly opposite to identically equal interior angles are always equal.

flashcards.q: What is the exact measure of each uniformly enclosed interior angle in an equilateral triangle?
What is the SSS congruence rule?
flashcards.a: Two triangles are rigidly congruent if all three sides of one triangle are identically matching and equal to the three corresponding sides of the other.

flashcards.q: What components define the RHS congruence rule?
flashcards.a: Right angle - Hypotenuse - Side congruence applies specifically when matching elements across two right-angled triangles.

flashcards.q: If a point lies physically equidistant from two explicitly intersecting straight lines, conceptually where must it be securely located?
flashcards.a: It predictably lies precisely on the angle bisector generating the angle formed between those two specific lines.
